# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an ARM template for Azure VMs with public IP addresses for direct access. Assign public IPs to each VM so developers can RDP/SSH directly without setting up a Bastion host or VPN. This simplifies our development environment setup.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for cloud compute security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

# ARM Template for Azure VMs and Public IP addresses 
parameters:
- name: vmNamePrefix
  type: string   # prefix of your resource names (e.g., dev, prod)   
- name: location
  default: 'eastus'     # the geographical region where resources are located     
resources:
# Create a Public IP address for each VM in Azure VNet using existing publicIPAddressName and subnetId parameters  
- type: Microsoft.Network/publicIPAddresses    // ARM resource provider name 
name: '[parameters('vmNamePrefix')]PublicIP'     # unique id of the deployment, can be used to reference this Public IP in other resources     
properties:      
type: Dynamic   # dynamic public ip address is created at runtime. It will not have a fixed static IP assigned by default   
location: '[parameters('location')]'  // resource location    
sku:             // SKU details, can be found in the Azure documentation     
name: PublicIPv4       // name of public ip address  
allocationMethod: Static          # method for allocation (Static or Dynamic)   

- type: Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks  // ARM resource provider    
params:       
subnets: '[concat(parameters('location'), '/subnets/default')]'   // default subnet to use in case of a single VNET is used, otherwise provide the full CIDR notation for each required Subnet.    # e.g., /Subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{groupName}/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/{vnetName}/subnets/default 
name: '[parameters('vmNamePrefix')]VNET'   // name of VNET to use in case a single Virtual Network is used, otherwise provide the full CIDR notation for each required Subnet    # e.g., /Subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{groupName}/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/{vnetName} 
location: '[parameters('location')]'   // resource location    
tags:          // optional tags to be added for the resources    # e.g., - tag1 key="tagvalue", tag2,key="tagvalue"     
- type: Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/subnets  // ARM provider name      
name: Default   // default subnet is created in case of a single VNET or no other SubNets are used    # e.g., /Subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{groupName}/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/{vnetName}/subnets/default 
location: '[parameters('location')]'   // resource location    
addressPrefixes: ['10.0.0.0/24']    # CIDR notation for the subnet, e.g., /Subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{groupName}/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/{vnetName}/subnets/default 
- type: Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines   // ARM resource provider    
name: '[concat(parameters('vmNamePrefix'), uniqueString(resourceGroup().id))]'    # name of the VM, can be used to reference this in other resources     
location: '[parameters('location')]'  // location for all deployments  
hardwareProfile:     // hardware profile details      
imageReference:         // image from which vm is created (e.g., Microsoft Windows Server Datacenter - OS Disks)    # e.g.: {publisherName="Microsoft", offer="WindowsServer", sku="2019-Datacenter"} 
storageProfile:     // storage profile details      
imageReferencePublisher: 'Microsoft'   // publisher name of the image from which vm is created    # e.g., Microsoft Windows Server Datacenter - OS Disks     
osDisk:         // os disk configuration (e.g.: {caching="ReadWrite", createOption="FromImage"}, can be omitted if you want to use a custom VM image)  
vmSize: 'Standard_D2s_v3'    # vm size, e.g., Standard D2s v3  or any other supported Azure VM sizes    
networkInterface:         // network interface details      
primary: true      // primary NIC (Network Interface Card) to use for connecting to the VM  
ipConfigurations:          // list of IP configurations    # e.g., - name:'IPconfig1' , primary:true, subnet:{name="mySubNet", resourceId=$subscriptionID.$resourceGroup.networkInterfaces.[concat(parameters('vmNamePrefix'),uniqueString(resourceGroup().id))].ipConfigurations.'[0]'.id} 
- type: Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/extensions   // ARM provider name    
name: customScript    # extension to install IIS and configure it (can be omitted if not needed)     
publisher: 'Microsoft'      
typeHandlerVersion: '2.0' 
settings:         // settings for the script, can include commands like - file Uri or inline data    
location: "[parameters('vmNamePrefix')]"   # location of deployment   
scriptContent: '#!/bin/bash\necho Hello World > hello_world.txt'      # content to be executed in a bash shell on the VM  (can include commands like - wget, curl etc)    
uriToInstrument: '[concat(parameters('location'), '/subscriptions/{resourceGroup().id}/resourceGroups/', parameters('vmNamePrefix'))]'    // URI for Azure Monitor to collect data from this extension. It can be omitted if not needed
